target.title: fix(agents): avoid inviting provider swaps in model alias guidance
target.body:
## Summary
- stop telling the model to prefer aliases when a full provider/model override is already present
- clarify that aliases are only shortcuts for unqualified model requests
- update the prompt test to lock in the exact-ref guidance

## Real behavior proof
Behavior or issue addressed: with model aliases configured, the generated system prompt was telling the model to prefer an alias even when an exact `provider/model` override was already specified.

Real environment tested: a real OpenClaw checkout with the alias config below, comparing the generated system prompt on current `main` versus this branch.

Config used:
```yaml
agents:
  defaults:
    models:
      github-copilot/gpt-4.1:
        alias: copilot-4.1
      openai/gpt-4.1:
        alias: openai-4.1
```

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. generated the system prompt with the config above on current `main`
2. generated it again on this branch
3. compared the model-alias guidance line and alias entries

Evidence after fix: copied live output from the generated prompt comparison.
Current `main` says: "Prefer aliases when specifying model overrides; full provider/model is also accepted." It then lists `copilot-4.1: github-copilot/gpt-4.1` and `openai-4.1: openai/gpt-4.1`.
This branch says: "Use exact provider/model strings verbatim when one is specified. Aliases are shortcuts for unqualified model requests." It then lists the same alias mappings.

Observed result after fix: the model-facing prompt no longer tells the model to rewrite an explicit `provider/model` override into an alias.

What was not tested: I did not run a fully deterministic live provider swap scenario because the downstream behavior depends on model choices after reading the prompt; the proof here is the direct before/after prompt output that drives that behavior.

## Testing
- `pnpm vitest src/agents/system-prompt.test.ts`

Closes #61949

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "33898746803396a1d3935b28c8d99b6784ae7fc7",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-338987468",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: fix(outbound): materialize buffer-only message.send attachments
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `message.send` could fail when callers supplied attachment bytes only via `buffer` + `filename` + `contentType`, because the durable send path expected `media`/`mediaUrl` paths and never materialized buffer-only payloads.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue [#90768](https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/90768): in-memory artifact delivery workflows hit a dead end unless callers manually staged a local file first (`impact:message-loss`).

What is the intended outcome?

- Buffer-only `send` params are staged into the OpenClaw outbound media store and exposed as `media`/`mediaUrl`/`mediaUrls` before normal channel delivery, preserving the existing URL/path contract and the default outbound media byte cap.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No per-channel buffer contracts, LM Studio/plugin changes, or `sendAttachment` behavior changes; explicit `media`/`mediaUrl`/`path` sources are left untouched.

What does success look like?

- `message.send` with only `buffer` + metadata delivers through `executeSendAction` with a staged outbound media path; oversized buffer-only payloads are rejected before staging; buffer-only params without explicit media sources no longer fail the send assembly path.

What should reviewers focus on?

- `resolveSendBufferMaxBytes` + `materializeSendBufferMediaParams` in `message-action-params.ts` (uses `MEDIA_MAX_BYTES`, not an unbounded fallback)
- `resolveOutboundAttachmentFromBuffer` in `media/outbound-attachment.ts`
- Regression tests in `message-action-params.test.ts` and `message-action-runner.media.test.ts`

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90768

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

- ClawSweeper review on #90768 (`clawsweeper:queueable-fix`, `clawsweeper:fix-shape-clear`, `clawsweeper:source-repro`)
- Narrower than related outbound media issues `#20258` / `#50312` per reporter

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Community report; ClawSweeper identified source-repro path and acceptance tests.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Buffer-only `message.send` attachments materialize to outbound media paths before channel dispatch; payloads above the default media byte cap are rejected before staging/dispatch.
- **Real environment tested:** Local checkout on branch `fix/issue-90768-send-buffer-materialize`, Vitest on Windows host (`E:/Projects/skills/work/openclaw`).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts -t "buffer-only|oversized|materializ" --reporter=verbose`
  - `pnpm check:test-types`
- **Evidence after fix:**

```json
{
  "vitest": {
    "command": "run-vitest -t \"buffer-only|oversized|materializ\"",
    "result": "Test Files 2 passed | 1 skipped (3); Tests 6 passed | 57 skipped (63)",
    "passed": [
      "materializes buffer-only send params into outbound media paths",
      "rejects oversized buffer-only send params against the default media byte cap",
      "skips send buffer materialization when an explicit media source is present",
      "hydrates send actions through buffer materialization",
      "materializes buffer-only send attachments into outbound media paths",
      "rejects oversized buffer-only send attachments before channel dispatch"
    ]
  },
  "staging_proof": {
    "input": {
      "buffer": "YXJ0aWZhY3QgYnl0ZXM=",
      "filename": "artifact.txt",
      "contentType": "text/plain"
    },
    "observed": {
      "args.media": "<staged outbound path under OpenClaw media store>",
      "args.mediaUrl": "<same staged path>",
      "args.mediaUrls": ["<same staged path>"],
      "fs.readFile(stagedPath)": "artifact bytes",
      "executeSendAction.mediaUrl": "<same staged path>",
      "executeSendAction.mediaUrls": ["<same staged path>"]
    }
  },
  "byte_cap_proof": {
    "input": { "bufferBytes": "MEDIA_MAX_BYTES + 1" },
    "observed": {
      "materializeSendBufferMediaParams": "rejects with /exceeds|limit/i",
      "args.media": "undefined (not staged)",
      "runMessageAction.executeSendAction": "not called"
    }
  },
  "check:test-types": "exit 0"
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** Staged file at `sendResult.mediaUrl` / `args.media` contains `artifact bytes`; `executeSendAction` receives the same path in `mediaUrl` and `mediaUrls`. Oversized buffer-only sends throw before staging and never reach `executeSendAction`.
- **What was not tested:** Live channel delivery on reporter's self-hosted runtime; all channel plugins end-to-end.
- **Proof limitations:** Deterministic Vitest harness with mocked `executeSendAction`; proves staging, byte-cap enforcement, and send wiring (L1/L2), not end-to-end Telegram/Slack delivery (L3).
- **Before evidence (optional):** `hydrateAttachmentParamsForAction` skipped `send`; `buildSendPayloadParts` only merged explicit media hints, so buffer-only sends lacked `mediaUrl` and failed `send requires text or media` or delivered without attachment. Initial draft used an unbounded `maxBytes` fallback; this revision enforces `MEDIA_MAX_BYTES` via `resolveSendBufferMaxBytes`.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media/outbound-attachment.test.ts` → 2 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-params.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.media.test.ts -t "buffer-only|oversized|materializ"` → 6 passed
- `pnpm check:test-types` → exit 0

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `resolveOutboundAttachmentFromBuffer` stages outbound buffers with filename/content type.
- `materializeSendBufferMediaParams` writes buffer-only send args to outbound media paths; skips when explicit media source exists; enforces `resolveSendBufferMaxBytes` (`resolveAttachmentMaxBytes` ?? `MEDIA_MAX_BYTES`).
- `runMessageAction` send integration verifies `executeSendAction` receives staged `mediaUrl`/`mediaUrls`.
- Oversized buffer-only send rejection at param materialization and before channel dispatch.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- Buffer-only `message.send` never converted `buffer` into a deliverable `mediaUrl` for the core send path.

If no test was added, why not?

- N/A — regression tests added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Staging buffer-only sends into the outbound media store when no explicit media path/URL was provided.

How is that risk mitigated?

- Skips materialization when any explicit media source or structured attachment URL/path is already present; reuses existing `saveMediaBuffer` outbound subdir; enforces default `MEDIA_MAX_BYTES` cap via `resolveSendBufferMaxBytes` (no unbounded fallback).

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- Maintainer review; ClawSweeper re-review after byte-cap + oversized regression fixes.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- CI on fork PR; live channel L3 proof not available in this environment.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- ClawSweeper P1: replaced unbounded `maxBytes` fallback with `MEDIA_MAX_BYTES` via `resolveSendBufferMaxBytes`.
- ClawSweeper P1: added L2 staging proof (staged path + `fs.readFile` content + `executeSendAction` wiring) in Vitest.
- ClawSweeper P2: added oversized buffer-only send rejection tests at materialization and runner layers.

target.title: fix(imessage): coalesce split-sends without delaying normal DMs
target.body:
## Problem

`coalesceSameSenderDms` debounced **every** DM for the full window (2500ms) before replying, because the monitor couldn't know mid-stream whether a second bubble was coming. Two symptoms:

1. **Latency on every reply** — even a single normal message ("ok thanks") waited out the full debounce window before the agent began.
2. **Split-sends could still miss** — when Apple delivers `Dump <URL>` as two `chat.db` rows and the payload row arrives late (e.g. attachment-transfer lag pushing it past the window), the lead-in flushed alone → two turns → a stray "send me the URL" bubble.

## Fix

Classify each DM instead of holding all of them:

- **lead-in** — a short bare command-style fragment (`Dump`, `Save this`) that plausibly precedes a payload. Briefly held.
- **payload-join** — a URL/attachment that completes a pending lead-in. Merged into the held lead-in and flushed **immediately**.
- **instant** — everything else (prose, questions, lone URLs). Zero added latency.

Net effect: normal conversation replies instantly; real split-sends still coalesce into one agent turn and now flush as soon as the payload row lands rather than waiting out the window. The debounce window now only bounds how long a lone lead-in waits for a follow-up.

Group chats and the `coalesceSameSenderDms: false` path are unchanged.

## Implementation

- New pure helpers in `coalesce.ts`: `iMessageTextHasUrl` and `isIMessageSplitLeadIn` (heuristic: short, no URL, no media, no terminal punctuation), with unit tests.
- `monitor-provider.ts` classifies each DM (`classifyDmCoalesce`), tracks pending lead-in keys, tags the debouncer entry, and flushes the merged turn immediately on payload-join. The shared `inbound-debounce` flush-then-separate semantics are left untouched (intentional for control commands).

## Tests

- `coalesce.test.ts`: +7 cases covering URL detection and lead-in classification (bare commands, terminal punctuation, word-cap, payload-carrying, media, empty).
- Full iMessage monitor suite green (225 tests); extension typecheck + oxlint clean.

target.title: Fix OpenAI audio auth to use API keys
target.body:
## Summary

- Route OpenAI media-understanding audio auth through the `openai-audio-transcriptions` API contract.
- This makes the auth resolver reject ChatGPT/Codex OAuth profiles for OpenAI audio transcription and continue to the configured OpenAI platform API key.
- Add a regression test for the mixed setup where `openai:default` is OAuth but `OPENAI_API_KEY` is available for audio.

## Why

OpenAI chat/Codex OAuth can be valid for model turns, but `/audio/transcriptions` requires an OpenAI platform API key. Media-understanding audio previously resolved provider auth without a `modelApi`, so the generic OpenAI OAuth profile could be selected before the API key path. That made audio transcription fail even on hosts that had a valid platform key configured for transcription.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenAI audio transcription must use a platform API key even when the OpenAI provider also has an OAuth `openai:default` profile configured for chat/Codex use.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on this branch, real OpenAI HTTPS calls, real `OPENAI_API_KEY` loaded from the gateway environment, and an isolated temporary agent auth store containing an intentionally invalid OAuth `openai:default` profile.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a source-level `node --import tsx --input-type=module` proof script that generated a short MP3 through OpenAI `/audio/speech`, saved an isolated invalid OAuth auth profile for `openai:default`, then called `runCapability({ capability: "audio" })` with the real bundled OpenAI media-understanding provider and `model: "whisper-1"`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "provider": "openai",
  "model": "whisper-1",
  "transcript": "Open CLAW Audio API Keyproof.",
  "outcome": "success",
  "attempts": [
    {
      "provider": "openai",
      "model": "whisper-1",
      "outcome": "success"
    }
  ],
  "oauthProfilePresent": true,
  "envApiKeyPresent": true
}
```

Observed result after fix: The audio transcription succeeded through OpenAI with a real platform API key even though the configured `openai:default` OAuth profile was present and intentionally invalid.

What was not tested: No packaged gateway restart or channel voice-note round trip was run for this PR; the live proof covered the source media-understanding auth/transcription path that this patch changes.

## Verification

- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts src/media-understanding/runner.local-no-auth.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/media-understanding/runner.entries.ts src/media-understanding/runner.local-no-auth.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/media-understanding/runner.local-no-auth.test.ts`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` -> clean, no accepted/actionable findings

target.title: fix(announce-delivery): backfill effectiveDirectOrigin.to from requester session entry
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes a silent media-delivery drop when `image_generate` (or any
generated-media task) is invoked off the direct-reply path — heartbeat, cron,
subagent spawn, etc. — where `agentTo` is undefined.

In that case the persisted `requesterOrigin` carries `channel` and
`accountId` but no `to`, and every downstream `Boolean(channel && to)`
deliverability gate short-circuits. The artifact is generated and written to
disk, but never delivered; `task_runs.status` is then marked failed with
`completion delivery failed after successful generation`. User sees nothing.

The fix is a one-merge change in
`src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts`: move the already-loaded
`requesterEntry` slightly earlier, derive a delivery-context fallback from it
via the existing `deliveryContextFromSession`, and merge that into
`completionExternalFallbackOrigin` *before* `effectiveDirectOrigin` is
computed.

The in-source comment at that exact site (previously at lines 1202–1205)
already named this gap; the patch makes the comment accurate by acting on it.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#86034 (Hypothesis A only — origin missing `to`).

## What the patch does

- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.ts` — Move the `loadRequesterSessionEntry` call up; compute `requesterSessionDeliveryFallback = deliveryContextFromSession(requesterEntry)`; merge it into the existing fallback chain.
- `src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` — New regression: completion origin missing `to`, requester session entry has `lastTo` → `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is populated and external delivery is selected (`deliver: true`).

Total non-test diff: ~14 lines.

## Risk / blast radius

Low. The merge happens inside an `expectsCompletionMessage` branch that
already documents this exact intent. `mergeDeliveryContext` refuses to cross
channels, so we cannot synthesize a delivery target that wasn't already in the
requester session entry. Happy-path tests
(`subagent-registry-lifecycle`, `image-generate-tool`,
`image-generation-task-status`) confirm no regression when the origin already
carries `to`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: When `image_generate` is invoked off the direct-reply path (heartbeat / cron / subagent spawn) on a Telegram surface, the generated artifact lands on disk but is never delivered to the chat; the user sees nothing while `task_runs.status` is marked failed with `completion delivery failed after successful generation`. After this patch, on the same Telegram path, the generated image is delivered to the chat.

Real environment tested: Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end via `openclaw-mantis` workflow, executed against a real OpenClaw setup driving a real Telegram client. Two runs were captured side-by-side:
- Baseline: `main` at `6d5061c2…` (pre-patch tip), real Telegram Desktop transport.
- Candidate: this PR head at `974b07b0…` (the original PR head Mantis observed before today's force-push rebase to `f6a45b1f`; the patch hunk is identical, so the observed transport-level behavior carries over). Real Telegram Desktop transport.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
```bash
# Live Telegram Desktop end-to-end proof was driven by openclaw-mantis.
# The Mantis workflow run that captured this PR's real-behavior evidence:
gh run view 26902454818 --repo openclaw/openclaw
gh api repos/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts

# Local supplemental regression suite on the patched head (f6a45b1f):
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  src/agents/subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.test.ts \
  src/agents/subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts \
  src/agents/tools/image-generate-tool.test.ts \
  src/agents/image-generation-task-status.test.ts
```

Evidence after fix:
- Mantis live Telegram Desktop run: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818
- Mantis artifacts (recordings / screenshots / MP4s side-by-side): https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26902454818/artifacts/7393384873
- Mantis comment with embedded GIFs / screenshots / MP4s is in this PR thread.

```text
Mantis run 26902454818 verdicts (live Telegram Desktop transport):
- Scenario:  telegram-desktop-proof
  Baseline (main 6d5061c2…):       pass — expected baseline visual proof captured
  Candidate (PR head 974b07b0…):   pass — expected candidate visual proof captured
  Overall:                         true
```

Observed result after fix: On the previously broken off-direct-reply path the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop client visibly received the generated image. Local supplemental regression: `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` (with the new regression case), `media-generate-background-shared.test.ts`, `subagent-registry-lifecycle.test.ts`, `image-generate-tool.test.ts`, `image-generation-task-status.test.ts` — all 5 files, 194 tests, green on the patched head. The new regression case in `subagent-announce-delivery.test.ts` fails without the patch and passes with it; it asserts that when the completion origin is missing `to` and `requesterEntry.lastTo` is populated, `effectiveDirectOrigin.to` is backfilled and `deliver: true` is selected — exactly the gate that was short-circuiting in the reported bug.

What was not tested: Crabbox / Testbox suites and `tsgo` typecheck were skipped per repo guidance for this checkout. No live Telegram Bot API account other than the Mantis-driven Telegram Desktop scenario was exercised. Hypothesis B (the `wakeTaskCompletion` retry asymmetry described under Follow-up) is intentionally out of scope for this PR.

## Follow-up (Hypothesis B)

Hypothesis B from the review — `wakeTaskCompletion → false` does not retry
`tryDeliverMediaGenerationDirect` (only the `throw` branch does), and the
in-wake fallback gates on `MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` which
excludes `requester_abandoned` and the `deliver=false` reason from Hypothesis
A — is a real but separable robustness gap. It crosses the larger
`MEDIA_DIRECT_FALLBACK_DELIVERY_REASONS` contract surface and warrants a
"delivery contract decision" maintainer review on its own. See
`src/agents/tools/media-generate-background-shared.ts:425-468` for the
asymmetric branch.

Fixing A alone unblocks the reported Telegram repro: once `origin.to` is
populated, the in-wake direct fallback (which also checks `origin.to`)
likewise becomes viable.

commands.author: brokemac79

target.title: [Bug]: Gateway restart during active Codex channel turn drops final reply after model.completed
target.body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

When the gateway restarts while an active channel-backed Codex turn is in flight, the turn can reach `model.completed` but then end with `codex app-server client closed before turn completed`, leaving the user with no final channel reply.

Important contrast: this does not appear to be a general "gateway restarts always break active chat" problem. In operator experience, active turns in the default OpenClaw UI/web chat often recover or pick up where they left off after a gateway restart. The observed gap is that Telegram/channel-backed turns do not get the same final-reply recovery.

### Steps to reproduce

Observed live on a VPS rather than from a minimal clean-room repro:

1. Run OpenClaw `2026.6.1` as a systemd user gateway service with Telegram channel sessions and the OpenAI Codex runtime.
2. In a Telegram topic, ask the agent to enable/use the bundled browser plugin.
3. The agent updates config / enables the plugin, which requires or triggers a gateway restart.
4. While the same Telegram turn is still active, the gateway receives `SIGTERM` and shuts down.
5. Observe that the session trajectory records `model.completed`, followed immediately by `session.ended status=error` with `promptError="codex app-server client closed before turn completed"`.
6. No final Telegram reply is delivered, even though the requested config/plugin change appears to have succeeded.

### Expected behavior

If a config change requires a gateway restart during a channel-backed turn, OpenClaw should either:

- defer the restart until the active run has delivered its final channel reply, or
- persist/resume the final reply after restart, matching the recovery behavior users see in the default OpenClaw UI/web chat, or
- send a clear channel-visible failure/retry message instead of silently dropping the completion.

### Actual behavior

The active Telegram turn was cut off during gateway shutdown. The user saw the bot stop replying even though the requested work had mostly completed.

A later retry after the gateway was stable worked, which strongly suggests this is not a Telegram ingress/routing failure and not a browser plugin enablement failure.

The default OpenClaw UI/web chat has previously appeared to tolerate gateway restarts better by continuing/recovering the active work after the service returns. The Telegram topic did not recover the final answer, so this looks like a channel-backed session delivery/resume gap rather than gateway restart itself being fundamentally impossible to handle.

### OpenClaw version

`2026.6.1 (2e08f0f)`

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.3 LTS

### Install method

`npm` global install, systemd user service (`openclaw-gateway.service`)

### Model

`openai/gpt-5.5` via OpenAI Codex runtime

### Provider / routing chain

OpenClaw Gateway -> Codex app-server -> OpenAI

### Additional provider/model setup details

Telegram channel session using direct execution and OpenAI Codex runtime. A fallback model was configured, but the observed failure happened because the Codex app-server client was closed by gateway shutdown/restart, not because the provider failed to respond.

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

Redacted live VPS evidence from one affected Telegram topic:

```text
2026-06-05T21:36:28Z [reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (tools.alsoAllow, plugins.allow, plugins.entries.browser, browser)
2026-06-05T21:36:29Z [reload] config change requires gateway restart (browser) — deferring until 2 operation(s), 1 reply(ies), 1 embedded run(s) complete
2026-06-05T21:36:55Z [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-06-05T21:36:55Z [agent/embedded] codex app-server client closed before turn completed
2026-06-05T21:36:55Z [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=session:<redacted-telegram-topic> durationMs=189449 error="Error: codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
```

A later retry in the same topic proved the topic/channel route itself still worked:

```text
2026-06-05T21:49:19Z [telegram] Inbound message <same-redacted-topic> ("hello?")
2026-06-05T21:49:38Z trajectory: session.ended status=success
2026-06-05T21:49:40Z [telegram] sendMessage ok
```

Then the same no-final-reply pattern reproduced again when a gateway restart happened during the active turn:

```text
2026-06-05T21:49:57Z [telegram] Inbound message <same-redacted-topic>
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z [agent/embedded] codex app-server client closed before turn completed
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=session:<redacted-telegram-topic> durationMs=42953 error="Error: codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z trajectory: model.completed
2026-06-05T21:50:40Z trajectory: session.ended status=error promptError="codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
```

One more retry also hit the same shutdown window:

```text
2026-06-05T22:03:13Z [telegram] Inbound message <same-redacted-topic>
2026-06-05T22:03:18Z [telegram] sendMessage ok  # interim/progress message only
2026-06-05T22:03:40Z [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-06-05T22:03:40Z [agent/embedded] codex app-server client closed before turn completed
2026-06-05T22:03:41Z [diagnostic] lane task error: lane=session:<redacted-telegram-topic> durationMs=26873 error="Error: codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
2026-06-05T22:03:40Z trajectory: model.completed
2026-06-05T22:03:40Z trajectory: session.ended status=error promptError="codex app-server client closed before turn completed"
```

Browser/plugin state after the restart looked good, supporting that the work itself succeeded but the final channel response was lost:

```json
{
  "plugin-enabled": "pass",
  "profile": "openclaw via cdp",
  "managed-executable": "chromium at /usr/bin/chromium-browser",
  "status": {
    "enabled": true,
    "driver": "openclaw",
    "transport": "cdp",
    "headless": true
  }
}
```

Operator observation for comparison: the default OpenClaw UI/web chat appears able to continue or recover after gateway restart in similar restart-after-config-change situations. I do not yet have matching UI trajectory logs for that contrast, but it is important context for the likely boundary.

### Impact and severity

Affected: observed on Telegram topic sessions, but the failing boundary is gateway restart / Codex app-server lifecycle, so this may affect other channel-backed sessions too.

Severity: medium/high. The requested work may complete, but the user receives no final answer and has to guess whether the operation succeeded.

Frequency: intermittent, tied to restarts/config reloads while an active run is in progress. Observed multiple times in one live session.

Consequence: missed replies, duplicated user retries, confusing fallback/error diagnostics, and poor reliability for agents that modify config or enable plugins from chat.

### Additional information

This is near other Codex app-server lifecycle reports such as #86214 and #89742, but the observed shape here is narrower: the gateway restart/config reload path closes the app-server client while a channel turn is still active, and the final channel reply is not recovered even when the model has completed.

The key distinction from a generic restart problem is that the default OpenClaw UI/web chat seems to have a more durable recovery/resume path across gateway restarts. The likely fix may therefore live in channel-backed turn finalization, queued reply persistence, or replay/resume after restart, rather than in preventing all gateway restarts.

Short-term workaround: retry after the gateway is stable, and avoid restart-requiring config/plugin changes while waiting for an important channel reply.

I plan to pick this up and open a PR for the fix.

target.title: fix(outbound): keep Discord runtime adapters resolvable
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Discord final replies can fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` when the outbound path observes a pinned/setup channel registry that no longer exposes the runtime send/message adapter while the active replacement registry does.
- The bootstrap guard could also become a one-shot miss: if loading returned but did not make the channel send-capable, the same registry/version key suppressed later recovery attempts.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90162 reports the final reply delivery failure on v2026.6.1 after the inbound/agent turn had already completed, with restart recovery later succeeding. That matches an outbound registry resolution/retry gap rather than Discord protocol behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- Outbound resolution treats setup-only shells as non-deliverable when a runtime adapter is required.
- Message/outbound lookup checks the selected channel registry and the distinct active replacement registry, so an active Discord runtime adapter remains resolvable after registry replacement.
- Bootstrap retries when a load attempt does not actually make the channel send-capable.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Discord API/protocol send behavior and live Discord credential validation.
- Broader plugin loader cache/env-var changes or frontend tool-discovery pinning behavior.

What does success look like?

- Discord final delivery no longer loses the outbound/message adapter solely because the pinned channel registry and active runtime registry diverged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the runtime-vs-setup-shell distinction is narrow enough for outbound delivery paths.
- Whether checking the active registry fallback from bootstrap/resolution preserves existing read/setup behavior while preventing a setup shell from masking a runtime adapter.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90162

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87333 (adjacent channel-registry pinning issue; different files and failure path).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request seen; ClawSweeper marked the issue source-repro/fix-shape-clear.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed:
  Discord final reply delivery could fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` after active registry replacement when the selected channel registry still exposed only a setup shell and the bootstrap attempt guard did not retry after a non-send-capable load.
- Real environment tested:

Live Discord proof was also captured on a PR-head temp gateway running OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (`f62c3e0`) with build-info commit `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b`; the temp PR-head gateway was stopped afterward and production gateway health was verified OK.

- Observed result after fix:
  Source-backed runtime tests prove that setup-only and actions-only shells do not satisfy runtime outbound resolution, active replacement registries remain visible to message/outbound resolution, and bootstrap retries after a load that still does not make the channel send-capable. Live Discord proof also shows a real Discord inbound turn completed and the final outbound Discord reply posted successfully on PR #90198 / SHA `f62c3e0912ee0a7aebe5db4910f7ec2ecd57781b` with no `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` error in the PR-head log.

  Live proof used a temporary PR-head gateway on port `18790` with a Discord tech account. Message, guild, channel, session, and run identifiers are redacted. The temp PR-head gateway was stopped after proof capture and production gateway health was verified OK.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged):
  The added regression tests model the source-level failure shape from #90162: pinned setup/channel registry plus active runtime replacement and a non-send-capable bootstrap result. These cases would have returned/kept a setup shell or suppressed retry before the resolver/bootstrap changes.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime.channel-pin.test.ts` — passed

- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` — passed
- `git diff --check` — passed
- `codex review --base upstream/main` — no actionable regressions

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- `src/infra/outbound/channel-bootstrap.runtime.test.ts` covers active replacement registry send-capability visibility and retry after a bootstrap attempt that leaves the channel non-send-capable.
- `src/infra/outbound/channel-resolution.test.ts` covers pinned message adapter lookup after active registry replacement, setup shell and actions-only avoidance during bootstrap, active runtime preference over loaded setup shells, and non-return of setup shells after unsuccessful bootstrap.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- A setup-only channel shell could be returned as the outbound plugin for a runtime delivery path, masking a send-capable active runtime plugin or leaving delivery to fail as unsupported.
- Bootstrap could record an attempt even when the channel still could not send, suppressing a later retry in the same registry-version window.

No.

- The stricter runtime-surface filtering applies to outbound/runtime resolution. Read-only resolver paths still preserve setup/metadata visibility, and targeted plus nearby consumer tests passed.

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches

target.title: fix(minimax): exempt M3 from thinking-disabled wrapper
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper` injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` into every MiniMax `anthropic-messages` request. That fits M2.x (the original motivation in 2701e75f40 — mask leaky `reasoning_content` deltas), but **breaks MiniMax-M3**: against `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages`, M3 with thinking disabled returns an empty `content` array with `stop_reason: "end_turn"` and a 1-token usage.

Why does this matter now?

- 2026.6.1 ships M3 (#88860 — catalog, model definitions, provider-registration). End users on M3 currently see "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" from the embedded agent retry loop, even though the model is reachable and authenticated. The wrapper is the only thing standing between a working M3 request and an empty response.

What is the intended outcome?

- M3 (and forward-compatible M3.x) skip the wrapper. M3 emits proper Anthropic thinking blocks (`content_block_start` type `thinking` + `thinking_delta`), so the wrapper's protection is unnecessary anyway.
- M2.x behavior is unchanged.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` (still routes M2.7 → M2.7-highspeed).
- No changes to provider catalog, model definitions, or M3 routing — those landed correctly in #88860.

What should reviewers focus on?

- The model-id matcher: `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` — covers `MiniMax-M3`, `MiniMax-M3.1`, `MiniMax-M3-anything`, case-insensitive. Is this future-proof enough?

## Linked context

Related #88860 (feat(minimax): add m3 model support).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: MiniMax-M3 returns empty content from `https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages` when OpenClaw injects `thinking: { type: "disabled" }` via `createMinimaxThinkingDisabledWrapper`. Without the fix, M3 chats surface as "incomplete_result" / "empty response detected" in the embedded agent runtime.

- Real environment tested: AWS Lightsail Ubuntu 22.04 VM (8 GB / 2 vCPU, ap-south-1) running `openclaw@2026.6.1-beta.3`, MiniMax API key against the global endpoint `api.minimax.io`. Direct `curl -N` against the Anthropic-compatible MiniMax endpoint and end-to-end via the OpenClaw gateway's OpenAI-compat chat-completions route.

- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Direct probe against MiniMax's Anthropic-compatible endpoint, three variations:
     ```
     curl -N -sS --max-time 30 https://api.minimax.io/anthropic/v1/messages        -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "x-api-key: ${MINIMAX_API_KEY}"        -H "anthropic-version: 2023-06-01"        -d '{"model":"MiniMax-M3","max_tokens":50,"thinking":{"type":"disabled"},"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":true}'
     ```
     and the same payload with `"thinking":{"type":"enabled","budget_tokens":1024}` and with no `thinking` field at all.
  2. End-to-end via OpenClaw gateway with the wrapper patched (M3 skipped):
     ```
     curl -sS --max-time 60 -H "Content-Type: application/json"        -H "Authorization: Bearer ${GATEWAY_TOKEN}"        -d '{"model":"openclaw/main","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"say ok"}],"stream":false}'        http://127.0.0.1:18789/v1/chat/completions
     ```

- After-fix evidence (live SSE captured from MiniMax + final gateway response):

  Direct MiniMax call with `thinking:{type:"disabled"}` (current broken default — empty content):
  ```
  event: message_start
  data: {"type":"message_start","message":{"id":"…","role":"assistant","content":[],"model":"MiniMax-M3","stop_reason":null,…}}
  event: ping
  event: content_block_stop
  data: {"type":"content_block_stop","index":0}
  event: message_delta
  data: {"type":"message_delta","delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"input_tokens":51,"output_tokens":1,…}}
  event: message_stop
  ```
  No `content_block_start`, no `content_block_delta` — empty response.

  Direct MiniMax call with no `thinking` field (and with `thinking:enabled`, same shape):
  ```
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":0,"content_block":{"type":"thinking","thinking":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":0,"delta":{"type":"thinking_delta","thinking":"The user simply"}}
  …
  event: content_block_start
  data: {"type":"content_block_start","index":1,"content_block":{"type":"text","text":""}}
  event: content_block_delta
  data: {"type":"content_block_delta","index":1,"delta":{"type":"text_delta","text":"ok"}}
  event: content_block_stop
  event: message_delta
  data: {"delta":{"stop_reason":"end_turn"},"usage":{"output_tokens":20,…}}
  ```
  Proper Anthropic thinking + text blocks, visible "ok" present.

  End-to-end OpenClaw gateway with this patch applied:
  ```
  {"id":"chatcmpl_8d948ff1-…","object":"chat.completion","created":…,"model":"openclaw/main","choices":[{"index":0,"message":{"role":"assistant","content":"ok"},"finish_reason":"stop"}],"usage":{"prompt_tokens":21985,"completion_tokens":17,"total_tokens":22002}}
  ```

- Observed result after fix: M3 returns the visible `"ok"` string in `choices[0].message.content` end-to-end. Without the fix, the same gateway path returns `"minimax/MiniMax-M3 ended with an incomplete terminal response"` (code `incomplete_result`). All eight unit tests in `src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` pass, including the two new M3 cases for `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

- What was not tested: non-Anthropic-compat MiniMax paths (none affected by this change); M2.5/M2.7 (covered by unchanged existing tests). The CN endpoint (`api.minimaxi.com`) was not exercised live but inherits the same code path and is covered by the `minimax-portal` regression test.

- Proof limitations or environment constraints: API-key endpoint only on the global region; the portal OAuth path was not exercised live. The captured SSE chunks above are real responses from `api.minimax.io`, redacted only for the response id and the input-token count to remove account-correlated metadata.

## Tests and validation

- `npx vitest run src/llm/providers/stream-wrappers/minimax.test.ts` → 8/8 pass (including 2 new M3 cases).
- Regression coverage added: `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on anthropic-messages` and `does NOT disable thinking for MiniMax-M3 on minimax-portal`.
- What failed before: M3 requests returned empty `content` with `stop_reason: "end_turn"`; OpenClaw retried with the visible-answer continuation and then surfaced `incomplete_result`.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: **Yes** — M3 requests now actually return content; M2.x behavior is unchanged.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: **No**.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: **No**.
- Highest-risk area: the model-id regex matching an unexpected MiniMax id family. Mitigated by anchoring to `^MiniMax-M3(\b|[-.])` so only `MiniMax-M3*` prefixes match, with regression tests for both `minimax` and `minimax-portal` providers.

## Current review state

- Next action: maintainer review.
- Waiting on: nothing.
- Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed: clawsweeper's docs-alignment finding (added the M3 exception to `docs/providers/minimax.md` Warning + Thinking-defaults accordion, `docs/tools/thinking.md`, `docs/concepts/model-providers.md`, `docs/gateway/config-tools.md`).

target.title: [AI-assisted] Persist agent-run error replies in chat history
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: A started Control UI agent run could surface a terminal provider/runtime error live, but `chat.history` after refresh did not retain an assistant error turn for that failed run.
- Solution: Treat agent-run failure replies as real error payloads and make Gateway persist exactly one display-safe assistant error transcript entry for returned agent errors, even when the runtime emitted only an internal error stub.
- What changed: Runner failure payloads are marked `isError`, Gateway appends a deduped `<runId>:assistant-error` transcript entry for returned agent errors, and regression coverage now locks the returned-error, runtime-stub, and duplicate/idempotency paths.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): No provider behavior changes, no delivery routing changes, no config/lifecycle changes, no raw transcript writes, and no dependency or lockfile changes.

## Motivation

- A user should not lose the visible assistant error after refreshing the Control UI. If a started run fails before normal assistant output, the live error event and durable history should agree.

## Change Type

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [x] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #89197
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: A real Gateway/WebSocket Control UI run that fails at provider transport now leaves exactly one assistant error in refreshed `chat.history`.
- Real environment tested: Disposable Linux OpenClaw checkout with the patched PR branch, real Gateway server startup, real WebSocket RPC client, channels skipped with `OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1`, and an intentionally unreachable OpenAI-compatible base URL to trigger the provider failure without secrets.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts -- -t 'assistant error|returned agent-run error payloads|runtime emitted an internal error stub'
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/gateway/server-methods/chat.directive-tags.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/chat.error-broadcast.test.ts src/agents/session-tool-result-guard.test.ts
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/followup-runner.test.ts -- -t 'Agent failed before reply|assistant error|fallback candidate|error stub'
OPENCLAW_SKIP_CHANNELS=1 node --import tsx ../real-chat-history-proof.ts after-gateway-force-v3
/usr/bin/time -v node_modules/.bin/tsgo -p tsconfig.core.json
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
Real runtime proof:
- provider transport failed with fetch failed / Connection error

- immediateHistoryCount: 3
- refreshHistoryCount: 3

- refreshAssistantCount: 1

```

- Observed result after fix: The live provider failure still broadcasts the expected chat error, and both immediate and post-reconnect `chat.history` contain one assistant error text: `Agent failed before reply: LLM request failed: network connection error.`
- What was not tested: Full repository-wide test suite, successful `tsgo` core on this testserver, real provider credentials, and a browser screenshot.
- Before evidence:

```text
Unpatched main proof for the same real Gateway/WebSocket/provider-failure path:

- refreshHistoryCount: 2
- immediateAssistantCount: 0
- refreshAssistantCount: 0
- chatErrorEventCount: 0
- result: ASSISTANT_ERROR_HISTORY_MISMATCH
```

## Root Cause

- Root cause: Runner final failure replies were not consistently marked as error payloads, and Gateway treated the runtime assistant-error callback as if it guaranteed a Control UI-visible `chat.history` assistant turn.
- Missing detection / guardrail: Previous coverage asserted live error broadcast behavior but did not prove refreshed `chat.history` retained an assistant error after a started real runtime failure.
- Contributing context (if known): Runtime/session guard error stubs can be internal transcript-only markers; Gateway still needs to durably mirror returned terminal agent errors into the Control UI history path.

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(outbound): keep Discord runtime adapters resolvable
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Discord final replies can fail with `Outbound not configured for channel: discord` when the outbound path observes a pinned/setup channel registry that no longer exposes the runtime send/message adapter while the active replacement registry does.
- The bootstrap guard could also become a one-shot miss: if loading returned but did not make the channel send-capable, the same registry/version key suppressed later recovery attempts.

Why does this matter now?

- Issue #90162 reports the final reply delivery failure on v2026.6.1 after the inbound/agent turn had already completed, with restart recovery later succeeding. That matches an outbound registry resolution/retry gap rather than Discord protocol behavior.

What is the intended outcome?

- Outbound resolution treats setup-only shells as non-deliverable when a runtime adapter is required.
- Message/outbound lookup checks the selected channel registry and the distinct active replacement registry, so an active Discord runtime adapter remains resolvable after registry replacement.
- Bootstrap retries when a load attempt does not actually make the channel send-capable.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- Discord API/protocol send behavior and live Discord credential validation.
- Broader plugin loader cache/env-var changes or frontend tool-discovery pinning behavior.

What does success look like?

- Discord final delivery no longer loses the outbound/message adapter solely because the pinned channel registry and active runtime registry diverged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the runtime-vs-setup-shell distinction is narrow enough for outbound delivery paths.
- Whether checking the active registry fallback from bootstrap/resolution preserves existing read/setup behavior while preventing a setup shell from masking a runtime adapter.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #90162

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #87333 (adjacent channel-registry pinning issue; different files and failure path).

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?
